# Lantern

> Lantern is a governed app platform for institution-built and AI-built learning
> apps. It runs small learning apps through one trusted LMS integration boundary
> for review, approval, grading, and audit.

Lantern is not a general app hosting platform, an LMS replacement, or a
compatibility layer for every LMS variation. Prefer the smallest correct path
that preserves Lantern's trust boundary.

## Start Here

- [README.md](README.md): product overview, local setup, runtime model, and
  deployment notes.
- [AUTHORING.md](AUTHORING.md): human-facing path for creating reviewed Lantern
  app packages.
- [AUTHORING_FOR_LLMS.md](AUTHORING_FOR_LLMS.md): rules and package shapes for
  LLM-generated Lantern apps.
- [APP_PACKAGE_SPEC.md](APP_PACKAGE_SPEC.md): v1 reviewed app package contract.
- [BROWSER_AUTOGRADER_COOKBOOK.md](BROWSER_AUTOGRADER_COOKBOOK.md): canonical
  pattern for reviewed browser autograders.

## Runtime Contracts

- [schemas/app-manifest.schema.json](schemas/app-manifest.schema.json):
  manifest schema for reviewed app packages.
- [sdk/app-sdk.ts](sdk/app-sdk.ts): browser SDK contract exposed to app
  packages.
- [main.ts](main.ts): local Deno server adapter.
- [src/worker.ts](src/worker.ts): canonical Cloudflare Workers runtime
  entrypoint.

Apps run as signed, reviewed, browser-first packages. Generated or institution
authored apps must not receive raw LMS tokens, direct database access,
arbitrary outbound HTTP, direct grade writes, or server-side execution inside
the package.

## App Examples

- [examples/apps/template/README.md](examples/apps/template/README.md): starter
  package reference.
- [examples/apps/web-checkup/README.md](examples/apps/web-checkup/README.md):
  browser autograder reference.
- [examples/apps/typescript-ladder-game/README.md](examples/apps/typescript-ladder-game/README.md):
  JS/TS teaching app reference.
- [examples/apps/quick-study/README.md](examples/apps/quick-study/README.md):
  small activity reference.
- [examples/apps/chapter-4-asteroids/README.md](examples/apps/chapter-4-asteroids/README.md):
  game-style activity reference.

## Development Commands

Lantern uses Deno. Common commands:

```sh
deno task app:new --list-starters
deno task app:validate /path/to/app
deno task app:test-preview /path/to/app
deno task app:preview /path/to/app
deno task local:init
deno task local:bootstrap
deno task local:start
```

For TypeScript changes, verify with:

```sh
deno task fmt
deno task lint
deno task check
```

Run touched-area tests when they exist.

## Coding Guidance

- Keep the architecture simple and the dependency graph small.
- Prefer one correct path over multiple fallback paths.
- Validate and narrow untrusted data at boundaries.
- Keep TypeScript strict: no `any`, no unjustified suppression comments, and
  explicit public interfaces.
- Prefer Deno, Hono, Cloudflare D1, hand-written SQL, server-rendered HTML, and
  plain CSS tokens.
- Avoid React, ORMs, Tailwind, queue platforms, and fallback-heavy abstractions
  unless a change explicitly justifies them.
- Keep UI institutional, calm, accessible, and compatible with LMS admin
  contexts.

## Public Context Boundary

Use this root documentation, public schemas, SDKs, source files, and examples as
the public Lantern context. The `private/` directory is not part of the public
documentation surface and must not be copied, summarized into public docs, or
used to expose private strategy, infrastructure details, account values,
security notes, pricing, or internal operations.
